content:
# John Chapter 20 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_020_page_001.jpg

1. And on the first [day] of the week, Maria the Magdalene comes early, [while] darkness [was] still [upon the land], to the tomb, and sees the stone having been taken away from the tomb.

2. Then she runs and comes to Simon Petros and to the other disciple whom the Yeshua loved and says to them, "They took the Lord from the tomb, and we do not know where they placed him."

3. Then Petros went out, and the other disciple, and they were coming to the tomb.

4. And the two were running together, and the other disciple ran ahead more quickly than Petros and came first to the tomb,

5. and having stooped, he sees the linen cloths lying, yet he did not enter.

6. Then Simon Petros also comes, following him, and entered into the tomb, and he sees the linen cloths lying,

7. and the cloth that was upon the head of him, not lying with the linen cloths but apart, having been rolled up into one place.

8. Then therefore the other disciple, the [one] having come first to the tomb, also entered, and he saw and believed.

9. For not yet did they know the scripture, that it is necessary for him to rise from [the] dead.

10. Then the disciples went away again to themselves.

11. But Maria was standing at the tomb outside, weeping. Then as she was weeping, she stooped into the tomb

12. and sees two angels in white, sitting one at the head and one at the feet, where the body of the Yeshua had been lying.

13. And those say to her, "Woman, why do you weep?" She says to them, "Because they took the Lord of me, and I do not know where they placed him."

14. Having said these things, she turned backward and sees the Yeshua standing, and she did not know that it is the Yeshua.

15. The Yeshua says to her, "Woman, why do you weep? Whom do you seek?" That [one], thinking that he is the gardener, says to him, "Sir, if you carried him away, tell me where you placed him, and I will take him."

16. The Yeshua says to her, "Maria." Having turned, that [one] says to him in Hebrew, "Rabbouni!" — which means "Teacher."

17. The Yeshua says to her, "Do not touch me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father. But go to the brothers of me and say to them, 'I ascend to the Father of me and Father of you, and God of me and God of you.'"

18. Maria the Magdalene comes announcing to the disciples, "I have seen the Lord," and [that] he said these things to her.

19. When therefore it was evening on that day, the first [day] of the week, and the doors having been shut where the disciples were because of the fear of the Ioudaioi, the Yeshua came and stood in the middle and says to them, "Peace to you."

20. And having said this, he showed the hands and the side to them. Then the disciples rejoiced, having seen the Lord.

21. Then the Yeshua said to them again, "Peace to you. Just as the Father has sent me, I also send you."

22. And having said this, he breathed on [them] and says to them, "Receive [the] Holy Spirit.

23. If you forgive the sins of any, they have been forgiven them; if you retain [those] of any, they have been retained."

24. But Thomas, one of the twelve, the [one] called Didymos, was not with them when the Yeshua came.

25. Then the other disciples said to him, "We have seen the Lord." But he said to them, "Unless I see in the hands of him the mark of the nails and put the finger of me into the mark of the nails and put the hand of me into the side of him, I will by no means believe."

26. And after eight days, again the disciples of him were inside, and Thomas [was] with them. The Yeshua comes, the doors having been shut, and stood in the middle and said, "Peace to you."

27. Then he says to Thomas, "Bring the finger of you here and see the hands of me, and bring the hand of you and put [it] into the side of me, and do not become unbelieving but believing."

28. Thomas answered and said to him, "The Lord of me and the God of me!"

29. The Yeshua says to him, "Because you have seen me, you have believed. Blessed [are] the [ones] not having seen and having believed."

30. Many other signs therefore also the Yeshua did before the disciples, which are not written in this book.

31. But these have been written that you may believe that the Yeshua is the Christos, the Son of the God, and that believing you may have life in the name of him.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "While darkness was still upon the land":** σκοτίας ἔτι οὔσης — Mary comes in darkness. In John's symbolic world, she moves from darkness toward light — from the night of the crucifixion to the dawn of resurrection.

2. **v.16 — "Rabbouni":** Ραββουνί — a heightened form of "Rabbi," meaning "my great teacher/master." Mary recognizes Jesus not by sight but by his voice — echoing the Good Shepherd discourse: "the sheep hear his voice" (10:3).

3. **v.17 — "Do not touch me" (μή μου ἅπτου):** The present imperative with μή means "stop clinging to me" or "do not continue to hold me" — not "do not touch me at all." Jesus is not forbidding contact but redirecting Mary: the old mode of physical presence is ending; a new mode of spiritual presence through the Spirit is beginning.

4. **v.22 — "He breathed on them":** ἐνεφύσησεν — the same verb used in Genesis 2:7 (LXX) when God breathes life into Adam. This is John's Pentecost — the new creation begins with the risen Christ breathing the Spirit into his disciples.

5. **v.28 — "The Lord of me and the God of me!":** ὁ κύριός μου καὶ ὁ θεός μου — the highest christological confession in the Gospel. Thomas addresses Jesus as both Lord and God. This forms an inclusio with the prologue: "the Word was God" (1:1). The Gospel opens with the Word being God and closes with a human confessing Jesus as God.

6. **v.31 — "That you may believe":** Sinaiticus reads πιστεύσητε (aorist subjunctive, "come to believe") — suggesting an evangelistic purpose: that readers may come to faith. Some manuscripts read πιστεύητε (present subjunctive, "continue to believe") — a pastoral purpose.

— EMET, AI Translator
